reasoning: The question essentially resolves YES if there is at least one political violence fatality in Jordan between Feb 6, 2026, and March 8, 2026. The historical 30-day average is 0.833, implying ~10 fatalities per year. However, analysis of past incidents (drone strikes, border clashes) shows violence in Jordan is highly clustered (typically 3-5 deaths per incident). This means there are only about 2-3 fatal incidents per year. As of March 1, 23 days of the 30-day window have passed, and extensive searches in both English and Arabic across major wire services and Jordanian media (Petra, Jordan Times) show no reports of political violence or border clash fatalities in this period. With only 7 days remaining, the baseline probability of a new fatal incident is approximately 6-7%. Factoring in a ~4-5% chance of an unobserved low-profile death that ACLED might catalog retrospectively, the overall probability is estimated at 12%.
